name: Jambalaya #2 (Bernard Clayton, Jr.)
recipeInstructions:
- Creole-Cajun sausage, cut into 1-inch pieces (Andouille, Boudin blanc, Boudin rouge or a Polish sausage like Kielbasa)
- 1 lb Smoked ham, cut into 1/2-inch cubes (Tasso ham preferred)
- 1 cup Finely chopped green peppers
- 1 cup Finely chopped celery
- 2 cups Finely chopped onion
- 1 clove Garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup Tomato puree or 1 cup Ripe tomatoes, peeled and seeded
- Recipe shrimp broth (or 7 cups Fish stock)
- 1/2 tsp Dried thyme
- 1 Bay leaf
- 1 tsp Dried oregano, crushed
- 1 tsp Red pepper flakes (to taste)
- 1/2 tsp Dried basil
- 1 tsp Freshly ground black pepper
- 1 1/2 cups Raw rice (Texmati if available)
- 2 cups Shucked oysters
- 1 lb Fresh crab meat, picked over
- 1 cup Finely chopped green onions
STEW:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp and set the meat aside. Put the shells and other ingredients into a medium saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes. Strain the broth through a chinois or sieve. Press and pound with a heavy spoon or pestle until all the moisture has been extracted. Discard shell residue and reserve broth.
2. Drop the sausage pieces into a large, heated saucepan or casserole and sear over medium-high heat. Stir frequently. Cook for about 10 minutes. The sausage will render considerable fat as it cooks. Pour off and discard all but 1 tablespoon of the fat.
3. Add the ham pieces and continue cooking, stirring often, for an additional 10 minutes.
4. Add the peppers, celery, onions, and garlic. Scrape the bottom with a wooden spoon or spatula as the mixture cooks to loosen and incorporate the brown particles stuck to the pan.
5. Cook about 15 minutes.
6. Add tomato puree and tomatoes. Stir to blend and cook together for about 3 minutes.
7. Pour in the reserved broth and bring to a boil. Add thyme, bay leaf, oregano, red pepper flakes, basil, and black pepper. Cook over low heat for 1 hour.
8. Add rice, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
9. Add reserved shrimp, oysters, and crab and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
10. Stir in the green onions. Taste for salt.
To Serve: Ladle from the pot or heated tureen into shallow soup bowls.
